description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the elixir:1.7 Docker image with a postgres:latest service and sets the variables MIX_ENV=test, POSTGRES_DB=lunch_with_me_test, POSTGRES_HOST=postgres, POSTGRES_USER=postgres, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres, REBAR_CACHE_DIR=$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.rebar3. Define four stages: deps, build, test, and coverage. In the deps stage add an elixir:deps job that runs apt-get update && apt-get -y install postgresql-client, then mix local.hex --force and mix local.rebar --force, then mix deps.get --only test, caches ~/.hex/, and stores mix.lock and deps as artifacts. In the build stage add an elixir:build job that depends on elixir:deps, runs mix local.hex --force and mix local.rebar --force, then mix compile, and archives mix.lock, _build, and deps as artifacts. In the test stage add an elixir:test:credo job that depends on elixir:build, runs mix local.hex --force, mix local.rebar --force, then mix credo --strict; in the coverage stage add an elixir:coverage:coveralls job that also depends on elixir:build, runs mix local.hex --force, mix local.rebar --force, then mix coveralls.html, and publishes the cover directory as an artifact. All jobs use the docker tag, with the test and coverage jobs also tagging postgres.
